title: Updates On Wynn's $70M Settlement Over Its CEO Scandal
author: Financial-Stick-8500
selftext:
Hey there, I guess there are some WYNN investors here, and you all know about it. But this info might be useful for you. It’s about its CEO's sexual misconduct scandal they had a few years ago.

For newbies, back in 2018, Wynn was accused of covering up that its CEO (Steve Wynn) had a history of sexual misconduct with Wynn Resorts employees. It was also said that they hid this information to avoid more regulatory scrutiny and to protect the CEO’s position. Obviously, when this came out, $WYNN fell, and investors sued the company. 

Fast forward to today, Steve Wynn was recently fined $10 million and people who presented a lawsuit against him for this harassment situation settled last year.

For investors, the good news is that Wynn Resorts agreed to pay investors $70M over this whole situation, and the court approved it. So if you bought WYNN back then, you can check the details and [file for the payment here](https://11thestate.com/cases/wynnresorts-investor-settlement).

Anyways, did you know about this Wynn scandal? And, has anyone here been affected by this? How much were your losses if so?
